We recommended to the local chief executive to:
Instruct the JEV preparer to indicate the actual date when the JEV is prepared.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should:
Instruct the Local School Board to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget for the ensuing year, and its subsequent submission to various office, following the schedule of activities as detailed in Items 5.5 and 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to promptly submit the quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 on SEF utilization to the Regional Office of the DBM.

We recommended the management to:
Require the Accountable Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to submit a detailed breakdown of the ORs issued to the check payments totaling P1,187,009.39. Representation shall be made by the LGU to the check payors to validate completeness of the ORs issued to them. Otherwise, require the erring Accountable Officer to immediately remit the entire amount. 
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to further verify and confirm the validity of the remaining untraced cancelled receipts to establish any unaccounted collections.
Conduct internal investigation to determine the existence of any fraudulent scheme and impose appropriate sanction/s to and/or undertake legal actions as applicable.
Install a strong internal control system in the receipt, remittance and deposits of collections to detect and prevent the occurrence of irregular and doubtful transactions, such as but not limited to the following:
The complete and correct mode (cash or check) and details of the payments received by the revenue collectors shall be indicated in the OR.
The liquidating officer shall thoroughly check the remittances made by the collectors in terms of the denominations remitted versus the breakdown of the mode of collections reported in the RCD.
The liquidating officer shall check the completeness of the ORs issued to all check payments received by the collectors.
Prior authority and approval for the cancellation of OR. The reason/s for cancellation shall be indicated in the OR, including the series number for its reissuance.
observations.managementAction:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer (AMT) had immediately dismissed Mr. Felix Opecio from his duties as collector upon the initial knowledge of this issue. Consequently, Mr. Opecio filed his retirement last December 27, 2023. She also issued a demand letter against Mr. Opecio to provide a detailed breakdown of the ORs corresponding the check payments; otherwise, he shall remit the amount therein stipulated in the AOMs. However, he failed to comply such breakdown because he no longer has access to the system. | The AMT only requested contact details for COA to validate, no internal validation was made by the MTO.


 No report on additional traced cancelled amounts was submitted by the MTO. All replies made by the AMT was incorporated and taken into account. However, a variance resulted since the AMT already considered those cancelled ORs subject of other AOMs as validated despite absence of traced reissuance.

Thorough review of the taxpayer’s ledgers shall be made by the Municipal Treasurer first to arrive at the accurate figure of delinquencies and the final result shall be the basis in adjusting both the MTO and accounting records in order to arrive at the correct RPT and SET collectible balances at the end of CY 2019. Periodic reconciliation shall be made from time to time to enable reporting of correct balances of accounts.
Further, the Municipal Accountant is recommended to make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the identified reconciling items due to errors in Table 4 for fair and accurate presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements and eliminate the abnormal balances in its records.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant effected the necessary adjusting journal entries due to errors in Table 4 for fair and accurate presentation of accounts.  This was recorded on February 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the recording of every transaction is in conformity with the accounting standards by taking cognizance of the actual essence of the transaction to be able to record them in their appropriate accounts, regardless of the class of appropriation where they were charged. Any discrepancies between the budgetary records and the accounting records shall form part of the reconciliation of the Statement of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA).
Effect the necessary adjusting journal entries for the subject transactions by reversing the expense accounts used and recognizing the appropriate asset account in order to present fairly the respective account balances in the financial statements. Completed projects shall be duly supported with Certificate of Completion and ongoing projects shall be supported with Statement of Work Accomplished to properly document the adjustments made.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant recorded the adjustments on February 2020. And the 2020 transactions were recorded appropriately with their asset accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management the following courses of action:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately provide depreciation for properties acquired from CY 2014-2019 since data, such as date of acquisition and cost, are already available. The provision shall be supported with a lapsing schedule showing the details on the property, residual value, estimated useful life, and depreciation method used;
Require the Municipal Appraisal Committee to provide the Municipal Accountant the appraised cost of the counted properties representing acquisitions made in the year 2013 and below, including the appraised useful life of the said properties for appropriate valuation in the books and accurate computation of their depreciation; and
In subsequent years, strictly observe the provision of annual depreciation of all depreciable properties for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Accountant commits to compute depreciation of depreciable properties this years and the previous years to from with its book value.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended for the submission of the Report of the verification and validation procedures undertaken by the LGU Personnel and justification of the length of extension granted, which was not complied with.
It was further recommended that re-evaluation of the time extensions be made by management before final payment.

We reiterated the recommendations that:

Refrain from granting further cash advance to those employees with outstanding balances.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
Record subsequent disbursements for on-going projects in the Trust Fund in its appropriate Construction in Progress account.
Set up a CIP account for the on-going Construction of Visitor’s Information Center.
Coordinate with the Source Agency in taking up the appropriate asset account for the six (6) completed projects in the Trust Fund so that asset recognition can be taken up in the General Fund books.
Further analyze, trace and evaluate the status of infrastructure projects in the trust fund which were already implemented in the prior years so that any completed projects will be taken up in the GF books.

We strongly recommended the following courses of action:
Rescind the contract with Agri Component Corporation on the ground of a cumulative amount of liquidated damages of more than 10% of the contract due to the failure to deliver.
Require the BAC to immediately conduct re-bidding of the Cassava Chipper, and to
Fast track the negotiations on the procurement of the Hammer Mill and Feed Mixers.
